executive_summary: The Iran–US conflict has reached a critical inflection point following the reported killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in coordinated US-Israeli airstrikes on 28 February. Iran has launched retaliatory attacks across multiple vectors—ballistic missiles against Israel and US bases, naval strikes on US vessels, and drone attacks on Gulf states including Dubai. A transitional leadership council has been established in Tehran, while IRGC proxies (Houthis, Hezbollah) have pledged continued support. The 6h incident window shows 155 events at MAJOR threat level, indicating sustained high-intensity operations.
whats_new: Bahrain announces interception of new wave of Iranian ballistic missiles and drones on March 1 (QNA General)
Iran's security chief Ali Larijani threatens 'unprecedented' retaliatory strikes against US and Israel (Middle East Eye)
Two missiles launched toward Cyprus, British troops near Bahrain base narrowly escaped (Jerusalem Post)
Saudi and Kuwaiti Defense Ministers condemn Iranian attacks, indicating regional coalition alignment (QNA General)
Iran launches missile and suicide drone attacks on Dubai targeting Western tourists and influencers (multiple sources)
Death toll from Iran school strike rises to 108 per Iranian Red Crescent (Jordan Times)
confirmed_events: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killed in US-Israeli bombardment on 28 February — confirmed by multiple sources including Al Alam, Daily Sabah, Middle East Eye, SCMP
Iran's interim leadership council formed with President, Judiciary Head, and Guardian Council jurist — Ali Reza Arafi appointed (Middle East Eye, Daily Sabah)
El Al Airlines suspended all flights until March 21 and subsequently canceled all flights due to conflict (RIA Novosti)
Iran launched coordinated ballistic missile and drone strikes on 14 US military bases across Iraq, Syria, and Gulf region (multiple IRGC sources)
Bahrain air defenses intercepted Iranian ballistic missiles and drones on March 1 (QNA General)
IRGC Navy attacked US MST support ship with missiles in maritime incident (multiple sources)
Protests erupted in Pakistan and Iraq over Khamenei's killing with casualties including 10 killed near US Consulate in Karachi (Dawn, Geo TV)
UN humanitarian chief urges protection of civilians, UN rights chief calls for all parties to 'see reason' (Jordan Times)
Russia condemns assassination as 'barbaric act' — Putin offers condolences to Iran (RIA Novosti, Shafaqna)
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims 201 killed and 747 injured in Iranian strikes — casualty figure from single IRGC source, requires independent verification
IRGC claims 200 US military personnel killed in strikes on American bases — Iranian claim not confirmed by US sources
Exact death toll from school strike in southern Iran ranges from 60-108 across different sources, full verification pending
CIA reportedly provided intelligence on Iranian leaders' locations to Israel's Mossad — New York Times report, unconfirmed by US/Israeli officials
Houthis/Ansar Allah pledge full support to Iran and commit to 'promised conquest' — statement from Houthi Electronic Army TG channel
Ayatollah Naser Makarem Shirazi issued fatwa calling for jihad against US and Israel — religious decree from single clerical source

people_travel: El Al Airlines canceled all flights indefinitely — Israeli civilian aviation effectively shut down
At least 27 flights from Hong Kong to Middle East destinations (Riyadh, Doha, Dubai) canceled or delayed
British troops stationed near Bahrain base during Iranian strikes — defense minister confirms soldiers were 'several hundred yards' from impact
Civilian airspaces across Israel, Gulf states experiencing significant disruption
supply_chain: UAE air defenses intercepted drone over Abu Dhabi — debris struck residential building at Etihad Towers
Maritime incidents: IRGC Navy targeted US Navy support ship in Gulf/Arabian Sea region
Iranian missile attacks on Dubai's Palm resort and luxury hotel — commercial areas targeted
Saudi Arabia and Kuwait coordinating air defense as Iranian missiles target regional infrastructure
market_macro: Hong Kong government prepares for market volatility amid Iran-US conflict (SCMP)
Regional stock markets likely to experience significant volatility
Energy markets: Iranian attacks on Gulf states risk disrupting oil shipping lanes
Civilian infrastructure strikes in Dubai signal potential tourism sector collapse in UAE

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is likely to intensify over the next 24-72h. With Iran's transitional leadership council now operational and threats of 'unprecedented' retaliation from Security Chief Larijani, expect continued ballistic missile exchanges and IRGC proxy attacks across the region. Gulf states (Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE) will remain high-value targets. US forces across Iraq, Syria, and the Gulf face continued harassment. The killing of Khamenei may paradoxically unite Iranian leadership and populace against external aggression, reducing incentives for de-escalation.
escalation_risks: Iranian attacks on Gulf Arab states could push regional coalition closer to direct involvement against Iran
Missiles targeting Cyprus demonstrate Iranian willingness to expand conflict beyond Middle East
Hezbollah and Houthi proxies may launch coordinated attacks on Israel and Saudi Arabia, widening geographic scope
Iranian threats to use 'new military capabilities difficult to defend against' could indicate WMD escalation
Civilian casualties (school strikes, volleyball team) risk triggering international intervention or humanitarian crisis
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation pathways remain extremely limited in the immediate 24-72h window. The death of Khamenei represents an existential narrative challenge for Iran's regime, making immediate concessions politically untenable. UN calls for reason have been issued but lack enforcement mechanism. Russia's condemnation signals diplomatic isolation but not military intervention. A potential off-ramp could emerge if Iran signals willingness to negotiate through intermediaries (Iraq, Oman) and US-Israel accept a ceasefire framework, but current rhetoric from both sides suggests neither is prepared to accept terms that appear to reward aggression. The most likely trajectory is continued escalation until one side suffers a decisive military or political failure.
